figures.caption: Figure 4. Log posterior variance estimates presented across runtime (hours) for the Bernoulli GLMM with simulated datasets (Section 5.2). Columns and rows indicate parameter and sample size (n), respectively; for each panel, posterior variances were averaged over 30 different datasets.

Figure 3. Posterior mean estimates presented across runtime (hours) for the Bernoulli GLMM with simulated datasets (Section 5.2). Columns and rows indicate parameter and sample size (n), respectively; for each panel, posterior means were averaged over 30 different datasets. Thick dashed line indicates the true parameter values used to simulate the data.

Figure 1. The first 5,000 (left) and the last 50 (right) iterates before numerical divergence of the SGLD iterates run with f ′(θ) given by (1). Red line indicates θ0 = log(2) that was used to simulate the dataset.
Table 2. Table of results for simulated study in Section 5.1. Each row displays the 1st and 3rd quantiles from 50 different datasets.
Figure 5. Posterior odds ratios of fixed effects and random effects covariance matrix parameters with 95% credible intervals for Section 6.
